---
title: "Lookups & Performance"
---

These patterns are not micro-optimizations for their own sake. Each removes a
redundant operation or a copy that the clearer form makes obvious — the fast
path and the readable path are the same path.

## One lookup, not two

`HasComponent` followed by the indexer is two lookups for one answer.
`TryGetComponent` is one.

```{.wrong}
if (lookup.HasComponent(e)) { var v = lookup[e]; Use(v); }
```

```{.right}
if (lookup.TryGetComponent(e, out var v)) { Use(v); }
```

## Reference, not copy

Assigning `ValueRW` into a local copies the struct, mutates the copy, then copies
it back. A `ref` binding mutates in place.

```{.wrong}
var d = debug.ValueRW; d.Field = x; debug.ValueRW = d;
```

```{.right}
ref var d = ref debug.ValueRW; d.Field = x;
```

## The unsafe lookup write-back

{{< unity-type UnsafeComponentLookup >}} has no `GetRefRW`. It is read-modify-write
through the indexer, and it is constructed via the `SystemState` extension —
which means it cannot be built from inside `exec`.

```{.right}
var v = lookup[e];
v.Field = x;
lookup[e] = v;
```

## Squared length for comparisons

Comparing distances does not need a square root. `lengthsq` is the same decision
without the transcendental.

```{.right}
if (Unity.Mathematics.math.lengthsq(delta) < radius * radius) { InRange(); }
```

## Swap-back removal in reverse loops

`RemoveAt(i)` shifts every later element; `RemoveAtSwapBack(i)` is O(1). In a
reverse-iteration loop where order does not matter, it is strictly better.

```{.right}
for (var i = list.Length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
    if (ShouldRemove(list[i])) list.RemoveAtSwapBack(i);
}
```

## Cache the query

::: {.gotcha title="Rebuilding an EntityQuery every frame is waste"}
`CreateEntityQuery` in `OnUpdate` rebuilds the query each frame. Build it once in
`OnCreate`, store it as a field, and reuse it.
:::

## The disposal that gets dropped

Iterating a `ToEntityArray` directly in `foreach` leaks the array — nothing
disposes it. Name the array, index it, dispose it.

```{.wrong}
foreach (var e in query.ToEntityArray(Allocator.Temp)) { Use(e); }
```

```{.right}
var arr = query.ToEntityArray(Allocator.Temp);
for (var i = 0; i < arr.Length; i++) { Use(arr[i]); }
arr.Dispose();
```
